title: Micromeria monantha
intro: Micromeria monantha is a species of flowering plant in the mint family, Lamiaceae, commonly found in the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory problems. Bioactive constituents of Micromeria monantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which have been identified as the primary compounds responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Micromeria monantha include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Micromeria monantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is classified as a member of the genus Micromeria. This species is native to the Canary Islands. Within the plant kingdom, Micromeria monantha is further classified as a dicotyledonous angiosperm. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Micromeria monantha is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties.
